---
title: "ParkPrice"
author: "Trevor"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

prettyplot<-function(p1, xlab, ylab) {p2<-p1+theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 16)) + ylab(ylab)+ xlab(xlab)
p3<-p2+theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill="white"), axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth=1), legend.position="none", axis.text=element_text(size=10))}
#prelim to make a nicer plot


#Figure 1 right Drent and Daan model the linear function is F=n+cy. gaussian function is  normal. max is given from differentiation *Fig 5 in text)


computeMax<-function(n,c, omega) (sqrt(4*c*c*omega*omega+n*n)-n)/(2*c) #max fitness of an individual in initial state, n. 

fitness_for_any_y <- function(y, af, n, c, omegasq) {
  (1 / sqrt(2 * pi * omegasq)) * exp(-y^2 / (2 * omegasq)) * (af * n + c * y)}


library(ggforce)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
avec<-seq(-0.05, 0.2, 0.005)
maxy<-as.data.frame(matrix(ncol=1, nrow=length(avec)))

#for (i in 1:10) maxy[,i+1]<-computeMax(avec, 0.07, (1+i)/10)

maxy<-computeMax(avec, 0.07, 1)

opt<-data.frame(avec, maxy)
optmelt<-reshape2::melt(opt[,c(1,2)], id.var='avec')
p1<-ggplot(optmelt, aes(x=avec, y=value, group=variable, color = avec)) + ggforce::geom_link2(lwd=2) + 
    scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red")+ylim(c(0,1.5))
p3<-prettyplot(p1, "Nutritional state", "Displacement")


#fig 1 left
omegasq<-1
af=1
n<-c(-0.02, 0.09)
c<-0.07
y<-seq(-2,3,0.01)
y1<-n[1]+c*y
y2<-n[2]+c*y
envcurve<-(1 / sqrt(2 * pi * omegasq)) * exp(-y^2 / (2 * omegasq))
plot(envcurve~y, type="l", col="purple",  las=1, bty="l", ylab="Relative fitness", xlab="Breeding date")

F_nlow<-fitness_for_any_y(y, af, n[1], c, omegasq)
lines(F_nlow*8~y, xlim=c(-c/n[1], 3), col="blue")
F_nhigh<-fitness_for_any_y(y, af, n[2], c, omegasq)
lines(F_nhigh*8~y, xlim=c(-c/n[2], 3), col="red")
lines(y1~y, col="#0000FF66", lty=2)
lines(y2~y, col="#FF000088", lty=2)


#fitness
library(rgl)
n <- seq(0, 4, 0.1)
y <- seq(0, 2, 0.1)
f <- function(n,y) exp(-y^2)*(n+y)
z <- outer(n, y, f)*10

contour(n,y, z, nlevels = 10, xlab = "nutrition", ylab = "breeding date")

open3d()
persp3d(n, y, z,
      theta = 40, phi = 30,
      xlab = "nutritional state", ylab = "breeding date", zlab = "fitness",
      col = "lightblue", shade = 0.5, border = NA, box=F)


plot(exp(-y^2)*(2+y)~ y, type="l", las=1, bty="l", col="red", ylab="Fitness", xlab="Breeding date", yaxt="n", xaxt="n")
at <- pretty(range(y))                 # tick positions
lab <- sprintf("%.0f", at)            # format with 2 decimals
axis(1, at = at, labels = lab) 
at2 <- pretty(range(n))
lab <- sprintf("%.0f", at2)    
axis(2, at2, labels = lab) 
lines(exp(-y^2)*(1+y)~ y, col="purple")
lines(exp(-y^2)*(y)~ y,col="blue")


                                                                                          
```

```{r Jose mean}
library(GPBayes)

xh<-function(sigma_y,omega,sigma_n, c, a) {
  xi<-(sqrt(2)*c*omega^2*(sigma_y^2 + omega^2)) / ( abs(a)*sigma_n )
  y<-HypergU(-0.5, 0, xi^2)/xi
  g<-data.frame(xi,y)
  return(g)}
  

sigma_y=1
omega=2
c=1
a=1
sigma_n<-seq(0,1,0.01)


z<-sapply(sigma_n, xh, sigma_y=sigma_y, omega=omega, c=c, a=a)
colnames(z)<-paste("sigman=",as.character(sigma_n), sep="")
z

#xh(sigma_y,omega,sigma_n, c, a)
```



```{r max in wbar according to perplexity equation}

#note 

y_opt <- function(omegasq, af, n, c) (-af * n + sqrt((af * n)^2 + 4 * c^2 * omegasq)) / (2 * c)

fitness_for_any_y <- function(y, af, n, c, omegasq) {
  (1 / sqrt(2 * pi * omegasq)) * exp(-y^2 / (2 * omegasq)) * (af * n + c * y)}

#add error to an individual n and compute the fitness.
errorf<-function(n, omegasq, af,  c) {
accumulator<-list()
for (j in 1:length(n)){
y_error<-rnorm(100, y_opt(n=n[j], omegasq=omegasq, af=af, c=c), sqrt(vge))
fitnesses<-fitness_for_any_y(y_error, af, n=n[j], c, omegasq)
accumulator[[j]]<-data.frame(y_error,fitnesses)}
res <- do.call(rbind, accumulator)
res <- as.data.frame(res)
seldiff<-cov(res$y_error,res$fitnesses)/mean(res$fitnesses)
stddseldiff<-seldiff/sqrt(var(res$y_error))
yvar<-var(res$y_error)
ybar<-mean(res$y_error) 
heritability<-vg/(vge+yvar)
data.frame(ybar, yvar, seldiff, stddseldiff, heritability)}

stats<- function(n, omegasq, af, c) { 
y_max <- y_opt(n, omegasq=omegasq, af=af, c=c)
fit_max<-fitness_for_any_y(y_max, af, n, c, omegasq)
seldiff<-cov(y_max,fit_max)/mean(fit_max)
stddseldiff<-seldiff/sqrt(var(y_max))
yvar<-var(y_max)
ybar<-mean(y_max) 
heritability<-vg/(vge+yvar)
data.frame(ybar, yvar, seldiff, stddseldiff, heritability)}

#sigmasqy=1 kestrel
#omegasq=27*27 kestrel
#c=0.094 kestrel
af=1
#sigma_n=0.44 kestrel
#nbar=2.6 kestrel
sigma_n=0.5
omegasq=20
c=1
vg=0.5
ve=0.5
vge=vg+ve

nbar<-seq(2,3,1)
 sigma_n<-c(0.5,1) 
results<-list()

for(k in 1: length(nbar)) {
n<-rnorm(1000, nbar[k], sd = sigma_n[1])
#run with no error in switch point.
noerror<-stats(n, omegasq=omegasq, af=af, c=c)

#adding error to y
error<-errorf(n, omegasq=omegasq, af=af, c=c)

results[[k]]<-rbind(noerror, error)

results[[k]]<-cbind(results[[k]], sigma_n[1], nbar, omegasq, c, c("noerror", paste("error=",vge, sep="")))
names(results[[k]])[c(6:10)]<-c("sigma_n", "Mean_n", "omegasq","c", "errorORnot")}

#plot(fitnesses~y_error)
```


```{r estimating parameters from Daan data}
#external mortality (Fig. 15, see excel attached)
x<-as.data.frame(matrix(data=c(25,50,75,100, 0.8, 0.94, 0.86, 0.77), nrow=4, ncol=2))
colnames(x)<-c("days", "fitness")
x_approx <- rep(x$days, as.integer(x$fitness*100))
mean(x_approx)
sd(x_approx)
```


## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
